description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked wooden logs, featuring a steep gable roof covered in brown shingles. The roof is supported by visible wooden beams and includes a prominent brick chimney extending from one end. The cabin has a single main entrance located centrally on one of the shorter facades, with no porch or overhang. Windows are small, rectangular, and asymmetrically placed along the longer sides, providing limited natural light. The overall appearance is robust and traditional, evoking classic North American or Scandinavian log cabin architecture.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep gable roof, central entrance, brick chimney, and small asymmetrical windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central entrance, log construction, and the steep gable roof with visible wooden beams.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long facade with two small, asymmetrically placed rectangular windows and the uninterrupted expanse of the shingled roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the large brick chimney centered on the gable end and the solid log wall with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two small windows, similar to the other side, and the chimney visible at the far end.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, covered in brown shingles and supported by exposed wooden beams, featuring a single brick chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, rectangular windows on each long side, asymmetrically placed and not grouped, with no windows on the front or rear facades
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade beneath the gable
